backgrounds.displayName: Sir Splintersworth's Glorious Coastal Disaster
backgrounds.description: A heroic seaside battlefield made entirely of broken cannons, sad smoke, and questionable decisionsperfect for settling ancient grudges and modern paperwork disputes with fists.

backgrounds.displayName: Lord Velvetpants' Extremely Suspicious Mansion
backgrounds.description: A luxury mansion where the fountain cries, the statue judges you silently, and someone left a sports car running which is either a dramatic escape or the dumbest valet job in history.

backgrounds.displayName: The Polite Street of Extremely Impolite Violence
backgrounds.description: A peaceful lantern-lit street where everyone whispers respectfully, bows politely, and then immediately launches a spinning kick because tradition is important.

backgrounds.displayName: The Grand Prix of Poor Life Choices
backgrounds.description: A glamorous street race where the crowd cheers, the engines scream, and two fighters decide the best place for mortal combat is directly in the racing line.

backgrounds.displayName: The Midnight Karaoke Bar of Bad Decisions
backgrounds.description: A neon-soaked biker hangout where every fight starts with "I wasn't looking at your motorcycle" and ends with someone being thrown through a sign that says *WELCOME* very aggressively.

backgrounds.displayName: The Festival of Unlicensed Sorcery
backgrounds.description: A cheerful night fair packed with spooky lanterns, suspicious fortune-tellers, and rides that definitely haven't passed inspection since 1843 so naturally it's the ideal place for a perfectly normal fight to the death.

backgrounds.displayName: The Jazz Tram to Nowhere in Particular
backgrounds.description: A chaotic street packed with neon signs, grumpy locals, and one suspicious tram that claims it's a jazz club which explains the vibes, the confusion, and the sudden need for violence.

backgrounds.displayName: The Volcano's Customer Support Hotline
backgrounds.description: A lava cave where the floor is actively trying to kill you, the walls are screaming in rock-face form, and the lightning in the distance suggests the weather also hates you personally.

backgrounds.displayName: The Tree That Demands Respect (And Snacks)
backgrounds.description: A misty swamp dominated by a colossal treehouse fortress, where nature is ancient, watching you closely, and absolutely prepared to throw hands if you step on the wrong root.

backgrounds.displayName: Captain Reginald's Totally Not-Cursed Dockyard
backgrounds.description: A golden sunset harbor where the ships look majestic, the barrels look innocent, and everything feels peaceful which obviously means something terrible is about to happen in the next 3 seconds.

backgrounds.displayName: The Temple of Extremely Unnecessary Biceps
backgrounds.description: A glorious fitness palace where everyone is permanently flexing, the mirrors are emotionally intimidating, and even the dumbbells look like they're judging your life choices.

backgrounds.displayName: Platform 7: The Slightly Wrong Train
backgrounds.description: A sunny station where everything looks normal except the train clearly isn't leaving, the crates are suspiciously placed, and you just *know* somebody is about to get thrown onto the luggage cart.

backgrounds.displayName: The Market of 1,000 Opinions (and 2 Chickens)
backgrounds.description: A busy street market where everyone is watching respectfully, half the crowd is secretly judging your stance, and the chickens are clearly running the place like tiny feathered crime bosses.

backgrounds.displayName: The Dockside of Mildly Illegal Commerce
backgrounds.description: A gloomy night harbor where the boats are held together by hope, rope, and bad decisions, while the fancy skyline watches from afar pretending it doesn't know you.

backgrounds.displayName: The Aquarium of Aggressively Peaceful Vibes
backgrounds.description: A classy aquarium lounge where families admire the sea life, the staff pretend everything is normal, and two fighters choose to start a brawl while a manatee silently disapproves.

backgrounds.displayName: The Sunset Factory of Questionable Safety Standards
backgrounds.description: A massive refinery where the air is 40% steam, 30% rust, and 30% "we swear this is fine," making it the perfect place to resolve arguments with flying kicks instead of health & safety paperwork.

backgrounds.displayName: The Business District of Passive-Aggressive Doom
backgrounds.description: A crowded overpass where everyone is dressed for meetings, pretending they don't see anything until someone gets launched into the traffic signs and suddenly it's "team building."

backgrounds.displayName: The Underpass of Unsolicited Drama
backgrounds.description: A busy city underpass where the traffic is loud, the teens are judging, and the yellow car is just trying to exist until two fighters turn the crosswalk into a personal disagreement zone.

backgrounds.displayName: The Courtyard of Elegant Overreaction
backgrounds.description: A breathtaking palace courtyard where everything is calm, symmetrical, and expensiveso of course the universe demands a completely unnecessary duel right in the middle of the reflection.

backgrounds.displayName: The Dragon's Interior Decorating Dungeon
backgrounds.description: A terrifying palace where a giant dragon statue breathes fire like it's showing off, the floor looks allergic to footsteps, and every echo whispers "you really should've stayed home today."

backgrounds.displayName: Gate 13: The Boarding Call for Violence
backgrounds.description: A frozen airport runway where the plane is ready, the crew is working hard, and two fighters decide to settle their differences next to the landing gear like it's a perfectly normal travel activity.
